name: Make ready-merge integrate Goals before rebuild and review

failed_actions.message: The durable GoalRound foundation model imports and persists tools::host::git_worktrees::MergeResult, coupling Foundation state to a host adapter type.
Ready Merge Git processes lack the workflow execution_id, so workflow cancellation cannot target fetch, merge, or push; cancelled work can continue integrating.
failed_actions.rule: Run long-running Git work through supervised processes with explicit ownership, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Integration validates node, status, round, and artifact strings but not the active claim, execution, or state revision. Additionally, candidate c9afedb7 is not descended from recorded base 749923d9, so this round is already stale and omits intervening integrated work.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel work through workflow claims, ownership, revisions, and isolated Git branches or worktrees.

failed_actions.message: The concurrency test uses two identical in-process merger calls and does not cover replaced or cancelled claims, unequal revisions, or separate daemons; the reported multi-instance test also failed twice.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to concurrency, persistence, process, Git, and recovery risk.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed c9afedb7 (Make Ready Merge own candidate integration). Ready Merge now serializes exact candidate integration and configured-remote push, persists idempotent merge evidence, recovers safely after retry/restart/push failure, then runs optional rebuild on the integrated target and Quality in the round-pinned pre_merge or post_build order. Review approval only accepts the existing integration; decline requires a new auditable round. UI, CLI/API guards, docs, and tests were updated. Exact verification: fresh `cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1` passed 457/457; `test-full-workflow` passed 1/1; `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, and `git diff --check` passed; the two formerly index-racy workflows passed in the fresh suite plus 30/30 and 20/20 stress reruns. Separate `test-multi-instance-sync` failed 0/1 twice because supervisor-state synchronization deleted the first daemon's new Goal before reconciliation; retained Git history showed that state-sync deletion, outside the Ready Merge integration path.
rounds.prompt:
Correct the Goal workflow implementation so Ready Merge is the merger-owned queue that integrates candidate work into the configured target branch before optional rebuild and final manual Review. Preserve the existing configurable Quality timing instead of hard-coding one visual order.

The required workflow paths are:
- With Quality timing pre_merge: In Progress -> QA -> Ready Merge -> Rebuild -> Review.
- With Quality timing post_build: In Progress -> Ready Merge -> Rebuild -> QA -> Review.
Rebuild is optional in both paths; when it is not configured, record an explicit skip and continue to the next configured stage. Each Goal round must continue to pin its effective Quality timing durably so later setting changes, retries, and restart recovery cannot reorder an in-flight candidate.

Ready Merge is a queue that performs integration, not a candidate-preparation label. When a Goal enters ready-merge, the system must serialize integration into the configured target branch. Use the recorded target branch and exact candidate commit, honor the configured Git remote, synchronize safely, merge and push exactly once through the existing repository and cross-process coordination boundaries, and persist auditable merge evidence. Advance out of ready-merge only after integration succeeds. A conflict, stale candidate, ownership race, revision mismatch, or push failure must abort safely, preserve the candidate and evidence, and use the supported failed/retry path; it must never advance while merely pretending the candidate was merged.

After successful integration, run the optional target-app rebuild against the integrated target-branch checkout, not the isolated candidate worktree. Pre-merge Quality evaluates the isolated candidate before integration. Post-build Quality evaluates the integrated, rebuilt target after integration. Preserve visible provider, test, cancellation, and recovery evidence for either timing.

Review is the post-integration manual acceptance boundary. Approving a reviewed Goal marks it Done and must not perform or repeat the Git merge. Requesting another round or otherwise declining acceptance must use the supported auditable workflow from the current target state without duplicate integration or hidden history rewriting.

Keep CLI, browser, API, and background workers on shared product services. Preserve configurable target branches and remotes, repository locking, worktree isolation, multi-daemon ownership, crash/restart idempotency, exact candidate identity, and the dynamic UI workflow ordering driven by the pinned Quality timing. Correct transition guards, worker ownership, and recovery behavior for both timing paths.

Update all affected intent and capability documentation so Ready Merge explicitly owns target-branch integration, Rebuild is optional and runs after integration, Quality can run before Ready Merge or after Rebuild according to configuration, and Review is acceptance after merge rather than the merge trigger. Update misleading comments and surface descriptions as needed while preserving the dynamic UI ordering.

Replace tests that assert the target branch remains unchanged in Review. End-to-end coverage must prove both Quality timing paths; that the candidate is merged and pushed during Ready Merge before Review; that rebuild runs from the integrated target when configured; that the no-rebuild path advances correctly; that post-build QA runs after integration and rebuild; that Review approval does not create a second merge or push; that merge failures do not reach later stages; and that retry, restart, stale-revision, and concurrent-daemon paths cannot integrate a candidate more than once.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation signals only already-registered subprocesses before marking the claim cancelled, while Git children are spawned before registration. Cancellation can miss a newly launched Git process, and the signalled path can leave workflow capacity unreleased.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Ready Merge drops the coordination lock between fence verification and each Git side effect or evidence write. The existing-integration path also returns without a final fence, and operation success plus later workflow transitions occur after the last check, so a cancelled or replaced execution can still integrate or settle.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The 4/4 multi-process gate passes, but cancellation is tested only after the push process is visible; it does not cover launch or post-fence settlement races. Unequal revisions are covered only by a single-process unit test that directly inserts a claim, not by the required separate-process production path.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed 6d04fbab (Fence Ready Merge integration across processes) on replayed candidate dcae5e7a from current-main base a413fac2. Moved durable MergeResult evidence into the Foundation Goal model; supervised Ready Merge fetch/merge/push under the exact workflow execution_id with durable cancellation/failure/restart operation evidence; added atomic cross-process claim, execution, node, round, status, candidate, decision-version, and Goal-revision fences before Git side effects and settlement; prevented replaced/cancelled/stale workers from settling; preserved configured target branch/remote and pinned Quality timing; added separate-process cancellation/replacement/unequal-revision/retry/restart/exactly-once coverage; and fixed first-reconciliation state-sync baselines so separate daemons cannot delete unseen remote Goals after a failed sync. Exact verification: cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1 passed 460/460; cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow passed 1/1; cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync passed 4/4 including two-daemon synchronization, cancellation/replacement retry, restart recovery, and child-process production paths; cargo test --lib tools::host::git_sync::tests:: -- --test-threads=1 passed 16/16; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check passed; worktree is clean; and a413fac2 is an ancestor of HEAD.
rounds.prompt: Reconcile the failed Ready Merge candidate from current main while preserving candidate c9afedb7 and all prior evidence. Fix only the four recorded Governance violations: (1) move durable integration evidence types into the Foundation model so GoalRound does not depend on host Git adapter MergeResult; (2) run Ready Merge fetch, merge, and push through supervised processes owned by the exact workflow execution_id, with cancellation, failure, and restart evidence; (3) atomically fence the active claim, execution_id, node ownership, round, status, candidate identity, and state revision immediately before Git side effects and again before settlement, rejecting cancellation, replacement, stale revision, or unequal concurrent claims; and ensure the new candidate descends from the round recorded current-main base; (4) add production-path coverage using separate daemons/processes for cancellation, replaced claims, unequal revisions, retry/restart, and exactly-once integration. Re-run the multi-instance synchronization gate and do not report it clean unless it passes. Preserve configurable target branch/remote and both pinned Quality timing paths. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard the prior worktree, or bypass provider, authentication, cancellation, Governance, Quality, or human Review boundaries.

Recover the preserved Round 2 candidate from Governance failure without force-merging or deleting prior worktrees. Fix only the remaining Ready Merge coordination, cancellation, state-sync conflict, and verification boundaries. Required outcomes: (1) make cancellation and Git-process launch/registration atomic with durable workflow ownership so a cancellation before registration cannot miss a child, all cancellation paths settle the merger operation and release capacity, and failures retain a non-empty causal error; (2) keep the workflow coordination/fence valid through every Git side effect, existing-integration recovery, operation settlement, and subsequent workflow transition, rejecting cancelled, replaced, stale, or unequal-revision executions before they can integrate or settle; (3) on Git state-sync push rejection, re-read the fresh remote and recompute three-way conflicts against the persisted baseline before replay, so unequal same-path remote revisions cannot be overwritten; remove the supervisor-agent.json last-writer-wins exemption and preserve supervisor events, open issues, and provider/auth failures through an explicit semantic merge or visible conflict; (4) add deterministic separate-process production-path tests for cancellation before process registration, cancellation during post-fence settlement, replacement/unequal Goal revisions across processes, retry-time unequal state conflicts, supervisor evidence preservation, exactly-once restart recovery, non-empty cancellation errors, and capacity release. Keep configured remote/target branch behavior. Run the focused multi-instance and git-sync suites plus serial library, full workflow, cargo check, fmt, and diff checks. Do not merge or approve; leave the resulting candidate for independent Governance and human review.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed 80ca16b8 (Close Ready Merge cancellation and sync races). Made workflow-owned Git launch/cancellation and operation settlement atomic, held the Ready Merge fence through Git effects, integration evidence, recovery, and transition, released capacity with causal cancellation evidence, and replaced the supervisor-agent.json overwrite exemption with explicit semantic reconciliation/conflict reporting plus fresh push-retry rechecks. Added separate-process launch, settlement, replacement/revision, retry/restart, capacity, evidence, and exactly-once coverage. Verification: focused state-sync 19/19 passed; serial library 463/463 passed; full workflow 1/1 passed; multi-instance 7/7 passed; cargo check --all-targets, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, staged diff check, current-main ancestry, preserved-candidate ancestry, and clean-worktree checks passed.
rounds.prompt: Recover failed candidate 6d04fbab from current main while preserving that candidate and all prior round and provider evidence. Fix the three recorded Governance violations narrowly: make subprocess launch registration and cancellation atomic enough that cancellation cannot miss a just-spawned Git child and always releases workflow capacity; keep the active claim, execution, node, round, status, candidate, decision version, and Goal revision fenced continuously across each Git side effect, integration evidence write, existing-integration recovery path, operation settlement, and subsequent workflow transition so cancelled or replaced work cannot integrate or settle; and add separate-daemon or separate-process production-path coverage for cancellation during launch, cancellation or replacement after the last pre-side-effect fence and before settlement, unequal concurrent revisions, capacity release, retry or restart, and exactly-once integration. Also remove the supervisor-agent.json conflict exemption introduced in round 2: unequal durable supervisor snapshots must not be silently overwritten or hidden. Reconcile disjoint remote records while retaining and explicitly reporting conflicting paths, or implement an evidence-preserving semantic merge for supervisor event history and open issues; on push retry, recheck original base and local state against the fresh remote before replay. Re-run the focused state-sync tests, full library suite, full workflow gate, and multi-instance gate, reporting exact results. Preserve configurable target branch and remote, pinned Quality timing, candidate ancestry, worktrees, and all provider, authentication, cancellation, Governance, Quality, and manual Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard prior worktrees, bypass failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.
